title: "podman login" doesn't create auth.json file even if no config file exists
body:
### Issue Description

podman-login doc says:

> The default path for reading and writing credentials is `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json`. Podman uses existing credentials if the user does not pass in a username. Podman first searches for the username and password in the `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json`, if they are not valid, Podman then uses any existing credentials found in `$HOME/.docker/config.json`. If those credentials are not present, Podman creates `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json` (if the file does not exist)

It is supposed to create  `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json` when no config file exists.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Ensure `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json` and `$HOME/.docker/config.json` do not exist.
2. Run `podman login`.
3. Enter user name and password.
4. Check  `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json`.


### Describe the results you received

The file doesn't exist.

### Describe the results you expected

The file should exist.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

My SELinux is set to permissive mode and `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` is writable by the user, so podman should have the permission to create the auth.json file.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
This does work for me on Fedora rawhide.

@xuhdev are you able to successfully login? Can you retry this with `podman login -v`. That should print the auth.json path as well.

Strangely I can no longer reproduce now. Yes, I can log in.

I'll close this issue for now and reopen if I can reproduce again.
